summary: Added a first C implementation for `src/melee/lb/lbdvd.c`, raising the file report to 30.70% matched over 7.54 KB. The PR reconstructed the preload/cache machinery around `PreloadCache`, `PreloadEntry`, archive/raw-data allocation, async file completion, and preloaded archive lookup, while renaming several symbols to semantic names such as `preloadCache`, `lbDvd_CachePreloadedFile`, and `lbDvd_GetPreloadedArchive`. It also updated dependent headers, symbols, scratch statuses, and assembly callsite labels across game, stage, and lb assembly files.

key_files.role: New 418-line C implementation for part of lbdvd preload/cache logic.
Assembly labels updated to match semantic symbol names and local BSS rename.
Expanded lbdvd declarations, callback typedef, and address-commented prototypes.
Reordered and address-annotated lbfile declarations used by lbdvd.
Added heap helper prototypes and corrected `lbHeap_80015CA8` argument type to `u32*`.
Added `efAsync_8006730C` prototype for effect archive preload initialization.
Address-annotated stage DAT prototypes used by lbdvd archive initialization.
Adjusted heap-free casts from `u8*` to `u32*` to match updated heap API.
Renamed lbdvd symbols and adjusted sizes/scopes/data annotations.
Converted relevant scratch entries from percentage labels to `OK`.

what_changed: Created `src/melee/lb/lbdvd.c` with local structs for `HSD_AllocEntry`, `PreloadCacheSceneEntry`, `PreloadEntry`, `PreloadCacheScene`, and `PreloadCache`.

Implemented or partially implemented lbdvd routines including `lbDvd_800174BC`, `lbDvd_800174E8`, `lbDvd_80017598`, `lbDvd_80017740`, `lbDvd_CachePreloadedFile`, `lbDvd_80017CC4`, `lbDvd_80017E64`, `lbDvd_GetPreloadedArchive`, `lbDvd_8001819C`, and `lbDvd_80018C6C`.
Renamed the BSS object `lbDvd_80432078` to local `preloadCache` at `0x80432078` with size `0x978`.

Renamed `lbDvd_80017AB0` to `lbDvd_CachePreloadedFile` and `lbDvd_80017EBC` to `lbDvd_GetPreloadedArchive`; assembly callsites in `gm_1A36.s`, `grstadium.s`, `lb_00F9.s`, and `lbdvd.s` were updated accordingly.
Added archive-type dispatch in `lbDvd_GetPreloadedArchive`: type 2 calls `lbArchive_InitializeDAT`, type 3 calls `efAsync_8006730C`, type 4 calls `grDatFiles_801C5FC0`, and the default path asserts.
Modeled preload-cache operations with `OSDisableInterrupts`/`OSRestoreInterrupts`, load-score updates, heap allocation/freeing, file reads through `lbFile_800164A4`, and debug assertions/reports.
Updated `symbols.txt` metadata, including splitting `lbDvd_80018F68` size from `0x2C8` to `0x198` and adding `lbDvd_80019100` at `0x80019100` size `0x130`.
Changed several scratch records for the imported lbdvd and nearby library functions from percentage status to `OK`.
smart_moves: The PR gave high-value semantic names to the cache singleton and key public operations (`preloadCache`, `lbDvd_CachePreloadedFile`, `lbDvd_GetPreloadedArchive`), which made downstream assembly callsites immediately more readable without changing codegen.

Reconstructing the cache as explicit C structs converted many hard-coded offsets in assembly into field accesses such as `entries`, `persistent_heap`, and `preloaded`, giving future work a concrete layout to refine.
Keeping `preloadCache` local in `symbols.txt` while updating all assembly references avoided preserving a misleading global address-style name once the C file owned the storage.

The lbdvd implementation pulled in exact subsystem dependencies through headers (`lbarchive`, `lbfile`, `lbheap`, `efasync`, `grdatfiles`, DVD/OSInterrupt/debug), making archive preload behavior visible across library, effect, and stage systems.
Address-commented prototypes in headers helped preserve decomp navigation and symbol/address correspondence during a large scratch-to-source import.
decomp_lessons: For large library files, first reconstruct the central state object; here, naming and typing `preloadCache` unlocked many functions that only differ by offsets into the same BSS block.
Semantic renames should be propagated into assembly stubs and config symbols immediately, so partially decompiled callers still document the discovered behavior.

When a cache API returns multiple data representations, model the discriminator early; `lbDvd_GetPreloadedArchive` uses a type switch to initialize normal DAT archives, effect archives, or stage DAT files.
Interrupt guards are part of the matching story for shared preload state; `OSDisableInterrupts`/`OSRestoreInterrupts` placement should be retained when reconstructing cache scans and mutations.
Header cleanup can be necessary matching infrastructure, not just style: adding missing prototypes and correcting pointer types allowed the new lbdvd code and nearby users to compile consistently.
Scratch imports should be normalized in `scratches.txt` after landing; this PR marked several previously percentage-scored snippets as `OK`.
naming_conventions: Use semantic names when behavior is clear: `lbDvd_GetPreloadedArchive` for the old `lbDvd_80017EBC` and `lbDvd_CachePreloadedFile` for the old `lbDvd_80017AB0`.
Keep unresolved functions on the `lbDvd_800xxxxx` address-name convention until behavior is sufficiently understood.
Use lowerCamelCase for local/static state objects such as `preloadCache`, even when the old symbol was an address-based global (`lbDvd_80432078`).
Header guards in touched headers were converted to address-style guards such as `GALE01_0174BC`, `GALE01_01615C`, and `GALE01_063930`.
Function declarations were annotated with address comments, for example `/* 017598 */ bool lbDvd_80017598(bool cached);`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed only labels/references in remaining assembly where the C name had become known, preserving addresses and instruction bytes.
Used a concrete `PreloadEntry` size of `0x1C` and `PreloadCache` BSS size of `0x978`, matching assembly patterns with `mulli ... 0x1c` and indexed access into 80 entries.
Preserved scratch-derived imperfect C where needed; the report shows a mixed file result with several 100% functions and several low/partial scores rather than a fully polished module.
Converted raw address symbol use (`lbDvd_80432078@ha/@l`) to `preloadCache@ha/@l` in assembly to align static storage naming with the new C source.
Added missing dependent prototypes instead of relying on implicit or placeholder calls, including `efAsync_8006730C`, `lbHeap_80015BB8`, and `lbHeap_80015D6C`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR dump, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that improve `lbDvd_80017740` or `lbDvd_CachePreloadedFile`, since this PR's report still showed partial scores for those routines.
Search for later refinements to the `PreloadCache` and `PreloadEntry` layouts, especially unknown fields like `unknown004`, `field5_0x5`, `field8_0xa`, and `x974`.
Check whether `lbDvd_80018F68`/`lbDvd_80019100` were later fully split and decompiled after this symbol size adjustment.
Look for review or later cleanup around header guard style `GALE01_xxxxxx` and address-commented prototypes in lb headers.
Search for later PRs replacing placeholder types in `lbdvd.h`, `efasync.h`, and `lbheap.h` with concrete types.
